title: ArcGIS Pro SDK Repositories Expert Analysis
description: Expert analysis and guidance on how to effectively utilize the three main ArcGIS Pro SDK repositories for optimal development practices

repositories.name: arcgis-pro-sdk-snippets
repositories.purpose: Comprehensive set of ready-to-use code snippets for ArcGIS Pro SDK add-in development

structure.organization: Organized by functional areas (Editing, Map Authoring, Map Exploration, Geodatabase, Layouts, etc.)
structure.content_type: C# class files containing code snippets within #region...#endregion blocks
structure.functionality: Demonstrates common tasks and workflows for each specific category

best_practices: Clone the repository locally using git clone command
Use Visual Studio's 'Find in Files' or GitHub Copilot's search to quickly locate relevant snippets
Copy and adapt snippets to fit your specific project needs
Pay attention to comments within each snippet for context, prerequisites, and threading requirements
Periodically pull updates from the repository to get new or improved snippets
use_cases: Learning ArcGIS Pro SDK patterns and best practices
Quickly implementing common functionality in add-ins
Reference for GitHub Copilot and other AI coding assistants
Experimenting with modifications to fit project requirements

repositories.name: arcgis-pro-sdk
repositories.purpose: Main documentation and conceptual guidance for ArcGIS Pro SDK for Microsoft .NET

structure.organization: Comprehensive documentation organized by functional areas and development topics
structure.content_type: Conceptual documentation, programming guides, API references, and tutorials
structure.functionality: Covers all aspects of ArcGIS Pro extensibility patterns

best_practices: Use as the primary reference for understanding ArcGIS Pro SDK architecture
Follow migration guides when upgrading between versions
Reference DAML (Desktop Application Markup Language) documentation for UI customization
Study the four extensibility patterns: add-ins, managed configurations, plugin datasources, and CoreHost applications
Consult the API reference for detailed information about each assembly
use_cases: Understanding the fundamental concepts of ArcGIS Pro extensibility
Learning about advanced topics like asynchronous programming in ArcGIS Pro
Migration between different versions of ArcGIS Pro SDK

extensibility_patterns.description: Custom tools and workflows that integrate directly into the ArcGIS Pro interface, providing new commands, buttons, panes, and dockable windows
Customize ArcGIS Pro's UI, startup, and user roles for tailored experiences
Integrate custom data formats into ArcGIS Pro for querying, analysis, and mapping as tables or feature classes
Build standalone console or WPF apps using ArcGIS Pro SDK for geometry and geodatabase operations, without ArcGIS Pro

core_assemblies.name: ArcGIS.Core.dll
core_assemblies.description: Provides CIM, Geodatabase, Geometry and Utility Network APIs

core_assemblies.name: ArcGIS.CoreHost.dll
core_assemblies.description: Provides Host.Initialize to initialize ArcGIS.Core.dll for stand-alone use

core_assemblies.name: ArcGIS.Desktop.Framework.dll
core_assemblies.description: Provides the application framework to include add-in contracts, DAML support, and base classes. This assembly must be referenced by every add-in

repositories.name: arcgis-pro-sdk-community-samples
repositories.purpose: Complete, working samples demonstrating key functionalities in various functional areas of ArcGIS Pro

structure.organization: 325+ samples organized by functional areas (Content, Configuration, Framework, Editing, Geodatabase, etc.)
structure.content_type: Complete, runnable ArcGIS Pro add-in projects with full source code
structure.functionality: Real-world implementations showing how to use ArcGIS Pro SDK in practical scenarios

best_practices: Download sample data from the releases page to run samples with actual data
Study complete implementations to understand how different components work together
Use samples as templates for creating your own add-ins
Examine project structures, configuration files (Config.daml), and resource management
Contribute back to the community by sharing your own samples following the contribution guidelines
use_cases: Using as templates for new add-in development
Understanding complex interactions between different ArcGIS Pro components
Validating your own implementations against proven examples
Contributing to the community by extending existing samples

learning_path: Start with the main SDK documentation (arcgis-pro-sdk) to understand concepts and architecture
Browse the community samples (arcgis-pro-sdk-community-samples) to see complete implementations
Use the snippets repository (arcgis-pro-sdk-snippets) for quick reference and implementation of specific features
Combine knowledge from all three repositories to implement complex solutions
development_workflow: Use the documentation repository as a reference for architectural decisions
Refer to community samples when implementing complex functionality
Use code snippets for common tasks and rapid prototyping
Cross-reference between repositories to understand different aspects of implementation

ai_assisted_development.copilot_integration: The snippets repository is specifically designed to be used as a reference for GitHub Copilot and other AI coding assistants. Structure your prompts to reference specific functional areas or tasks covered in these repositories.
prompt_examples: How to implement feature selection in ArcGIS Pro using the framework approach demonstrated in the samples?
Show me a code snippet for asynchronous geoprocessing in ArcGIS Pro similar to the ones in the snippets repository?
Provide an example of DAML configuration for a custom dockpane based on the documentation and samples?

recommendations_for_expert_developers: Combine the conceptual knowledge from the main SDK repository with practical implementations from the community samples
Use the snippets repository for rapid prototyping and common functionality implementation
Understand the threading model and asynchronous programming patterns specific to ArcGIS Pro
Pay special attention to the different extensibility patterns and choose the appropriate one for your use case
Consider performance implications when working with large datasets or complex geometries
Follow the security and licensing guidelines when deploying add-ins in enterprise environments
Utilize the CoreHost pattern for standalone applications that need ArcGIS functionality without the full Pro interface
